text: This is a twenty-two year old lady who presented with pain during menses. Ultrasound showed a small right ovarian cyst. Single incision laparoscopy was performed using the three-part technique. Both tubes were patent.
The surgeon opens the endometriotic cyst and releases it from the lateral pelvic wall. Vasopressin is injected into the area, followed by the cystectomy procedure to remove the cyst.
The surgeon coagulates visible endometriotic spots. The cyst wall is placed into a retrieval bag, which is then extracted through the umbilical wound. The wound is sutured closed.
